summary: Frontend-only review of the new sound-effects feature (3 SFX: room-enter, message, notification). Static code review of /app/frontend/src/utils/sound.ts, /app/frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx, /app/frontend/app/room/[id].tsx and backend endpoints used by the polling effects is POSITIVE — implementation is correct and consistent with the design. REST regression also PASSES (frontend HTTP 200, /api/auth/me returns 403 unauthenticated as expected, login + /rooms/:id/members + /messages/:id + /notifications all return 200). I was UNABLE to obtain a live runtime verification of oscillator calls because the browser_automation wrapper kept aborting the pre-navigation step against the Expo web bundle (initial bundle build > 10s wrapper timeout); the wrapper retried 3x and never executed my injected script (no stdout from any print). I stopped after the third attempt per loop-prevention rules. Main agent should run a manual oscillator-monkeypatch verification (script provided below) in a real browser or with a higher Playwright timeout, OR I can re-run once the wrapper / bundle starts cold-loading within the 10s preview-nav budget.
action_items: Main agent: please run a manual smoke in a real browser (devtools open) — log in as hosttest1780846544, open /room/6a258fd574a5efd0f4d3baf5, click once anywhere (to satisfy autoplay-unlock), then trigger the three events via the provided curls/mongo insert. You should hear the 3 distinct SFX. Also paste the oscillator-counter snippet (below) into devtools to assert frequency-tagged counts.

Consider raising the per-call wrapper navigation budget OR pre-bundling Expo web on container start so headless E2E is reliable. Right now testing-agent runs against /room/<id> are flaky because Metro first-bundle exceeds the wrapper's 10s pre-nav timeout, and the wrapper never recovers to run the script.
critical_code_review_comments: sound.ts is implemented correctly: Web Audio is gated by Platform.OS === 'web' (no-op on native, good). Single AudioContext singleton + one-time pointer/key/touch unlock listener properly handle browsers' autoplay policy. Throttle table (room-enter 250ms, notification 350ms, message 220ms) prevents burst stacking. Each player uses distinct frequency signatures (220-660 Hz sweep, 1318/1760/2637 Hz bell, 660→420 Hz tap) so headless oscillator-frequency counting can disambiguate them.

AuthContext.tsx polling effect is correct: seenNotificationIdsRef is seeded to a Set on FIRST poll (no sound), then on every subsequent poll computes prev-vs-new set diff. lastDmUnreadRef starts null → no DM sound on first poll, and only fires when strictly increases. Cleanup correctly clears both refs to null on logout and clears both intervals on unmount. One minor concern: if /messages/direct/unread/total monotonically decreases when the user opens DMs, that's fine — sound only fires on increases — but there is no debounce against the user themselves sending a DM to someone who then auto-marks read; not a bug, just behaviour to confirm.

room/[id].tsx polling effect is correct: seenMemberIdsRef and seenMessageIdsRef are both seeded silently inside loadRoomData() (the FIRST load) and only refreshRoomData() (every 3s) compares and plays. Both correctly EXCLUDE self (m.userId !== user?.id, msg.senderId !== user?.id), so a user joining/sending will not hear their own action. Refs are reset only via component unmount → if the user navigates to another room id the [id] dep re-runs the loadRoomData() and reseeds — correct.

Edge case to consider (not blocking): if a poll fails (network error) the refs are NOT updated, so on the next successful poll the gap will be treated as 'new arrivals' and sounds will play for items added during the offline window. That matches the design (you want to hear what you missed), but consider capping `newEntries` / `newInbound` count comparisons to avoid loud stacks (the throttle in sound.ts already mitigates).

Backend endpoint surface is intact — POST /api/messages/{room_id}, POST /api/rooms/{id}/join, POST /api/rooms/{id}/leave, POST /api/friends/request, GET /api/notifications, GET /api/messages/direct/unread/total all exist and were verified at the route level. No backend changes required for this feature.

success_rate.backend: 100% (REST regression: frontend 200, login 200, /api/auth/me unauth 403 as designed, members 200)
success_rate.frontend: code-review PASS; runtime oscillator-call verification BLOCKED by wrapper pre-nav timeout (could not be executed)

test_credentials: hosttest1780846544@test.com / TestPass123!  + scatter1@test.com  + scatter2@test.com (all TestPass123!). Room id 6a258fd574a5efd0f4d3baf5 (World Vibez).
seed_data_creation: None this iteration (the 3 users + 3-member room are still seeded from iteration 14). During the failed Playwright attempts I called POST /api/rooms/<id>/leave on scatter2 — but since my script never executed those calls in the browser context, the membership state was NOT modified (verify via GET /rooms/<id>/members before next run; if scatter2 is missing, just POST /rooms/<id>/join with TOK_C).
rca of the issue: Not a code bug — environment/tooling issue. The Expo web bundle's first compile on a cold preview URL exceeds the browser_automation wrapper's hard-coded 10s pre-navigation timeout. The wrapper retries up to 3x with progressively longer waits but in 3 of 3 runs it gave up and emitted a JSON-decode failure ('Script error: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)') without ever invoking my Playwright script (no stdout from any of my print() calls). Mitigation options for main agent: (a) reduce CI=true so Metro keeps the dev cache warm, (b) pre-build the Expo web bundle in supervisor's startup, or (c) instruct the testing agent to use a fast page_url and rely solely on its own page.goto with timeout >= 90000ms — but the wrapper's initial nav is unavoidable. Code-review-wise the sound feature is correct and should work in any real browser session.
